proof_obligations.statement: candidate operation maps observed inputs back into its claimed domain
substituting equal expressions preserves prediction and score
regrouping repeated composition does not alter the result
the system can find no-op and undo operations when a structure claims them
the system states whether swapping operands preserves or changes the result
multiplication or scaling distributes over claimed addition-like structure
division, inverse, power, and log forms declare excluded or positive domains
both sides of an equation use compatible units or explicitly learned dimensions
order claims identify where a relation increases, decreases, or reaches an extremum
piecewise domains cover the relevant cases and state boundary behavior
a transition rule proves a base case and repeated-step consistency
claimed coordinate-free laws survive transform, inverse transform, or basis change
every promoted form names a held-out setting that would break it

search_controls.anti_template_policy: families are allowed search moves, not answers; every promoted equation must win held-out scoring and state falsification conditions

complexity_budget.expand_when: heldout residual or disagreement remains after simpler families

search_controls.dimension_policy: new variables may be invented from residuals, but they must improve transfer, separate rivals, or explain a domain-limited failure
search_controls.proof_policy: attach the cheapest relevant obligation first, then escalate to algebraic structure checks when an operator is reused across contexts

algebraic_foundation_baseline.description: broad equation and algebra grammar is available with proof obligations

anomaly_repair_loop.description: failed priors create anomalies or repair experiments

arithmetic_rediscovery_probe.description: counting and unit arithmetic are rediscovered from public observations

autonomous_next_experiments.description: the memory notebook can emit concrete next experiments

broad_domain_curriculum.description: a multi-domain rediscovery curriculum covers core mathematical pressure sources

canonical_law_compression.description: repeated laws are compacted into reusable canonical summaries

claim_driven_planning.description: operator claims choose validation or holdout tests

domain_transfer_loop.description: the curriculum emits cross-domain transfer probes with falsifiers

domain_world_discovery_loop.description: generated domain worlds yield self-authored candidate equations and falsifiers

domain_world_transfer_evidence.description: discovered relation bases connect source and target math domains

executable_domain_worlds.description: each math domain has generated observations plus benchmark-only falsifiers

executable_operator_priors.description: representation proposals become executable generated operator priors

first_principles_adaptive_dimensions.description: primitive rules can lift residuals into new dimensions

model_disagreement_planning.description: rival theories can choose falsification probes

operator_discovery_claims.description: invented operators become proof-like discovery claims

operator_prior_feedback.description: generated priors are judged confirmed, weak, or unmatched

proof_like_evaluation.description: theories carry proof-like checks or certificates

representation_agenda.description: the loop proposes new variables, operators, or domain predicates

residual_to_theory.description: residual observations produce candidate theory families

scientist_harder_hidden_worlds.description: the loop selects harder hidden worlds for stress-testing laws

scientist_invariant_consolidation.description: repeated runs are consolidated into robust invariant law candidates

scientist_live_trace.description: live events expose what the scientist is seeing and doing

scientist_residual_experiment_loop.description: candidate failures or untested falsifiers design the next experiment

scientist_richer_equation_writing.description: the loop rewrites local equations into richer reusable equation grammar

self_authored_equation_synthesis.description: repeated discoveries are consolidated into self-authored equation templates

math_domain_curriculum.curriculum_policy: each domain is a pressure source, not an answer bank; a candidate concept must be rediscovered from observations, transferred across at least one bridge, and survive a named falsifier

domains.name: arithmetic and quantity
observation_tasks: count stable objects under permutation
compare more, fewer, same, zero, and one-more transitions
compose combine/split actions and check whether totals agree
domains.world_seed: finite objects that can appear, disappear, combine, and split

domains.name: algebra and symbolic equations
observation_tasks: solve missing values from balanced transformations
compose and invert operations
compare equivalent expressions on held-out substitutions
domains.world_seed: unknown quantities hidden behind reversible operations

domains.name: geometry and measurement
observation_tasks: measure invariant distance under translation and rotation
infer centers, axes, and boundaries from observations
compare coordinate descriptions of the same shape
domains.world_seed: points, distances, angles, boundaries, and coordinate changes

domains.name: calculus and change
observation_tasks: estimate velocity, acceleration, and curvature from finite differences
compare accumulated change against endpoint differences
separate smooth trends from boundary events
domains.world_seed: smooth and abrupt change sampled over time

domains.name: probability and uncertainty
observation_tasks: estimate frequencies from repeated samples
update beliefs after evidence
distinguish noise from deterministic residual structure
domains.world_seed: stochastic events, noisy measurements, and repeated trials

domains.name: logic and proof
observation_tasks: test whether predicates partition examples
build implication chains from observed rules
search for the smallest counterexample to a claim
domains.world_seed: statements, predicates, implications, contradictions, and counterexamples

domains.name: discrete structures and graphs
observation_tasks: discover adjacency and reachability
compare path composition and shortest routes
track state-machine transitions over repeated steps
domains.world_seed: nodes, edges, paths, neighborhoods, orderings, and finite state transitions

domains.name: symmetry and invariance
observation_tasks: apply translations, rotations, reflections, and relabelings
detect quantities unchanged by transformations
separate true invariants from coordinate artifacts
domains.world_seed: transformations that preserve or break structure

domains.name: optimization and extrema
observation_tasks: compare candidate rules by residual/error cost
search local and global optima
discover constraints and active boundaries
domains.world_seed: actions with costs, rewards, constraints, and tradeoffs

domains.name: dynamics and systems
observation_tasks: infer residual fields after a simple transition baseline
discover attractors, rotations, periodic forcing, and conservation
compose multiple laws and test held-out trajectories
domains.world_seed: interacting objects with hidden forces, phases, feedback, and conservation

domains.name: information and computation
observation_tasks: compare descriptions by compression and prediction cost
infer hidden state from partial observations
learn procedures that generalize across input sizes
domains.world_seed: messages, encodings, compression, decision trees, and algorithms

domains.name: higher-dimensional worlds
observation_tasks: invent new coordinates when residuals cannot be expressed in visible axes
project high-dimensional structure into observable views
test whether learned laws survive dimension changes
domains.world_seed: latent axes, projections, manifolds, and arbitrary-dimensional coordinates

domain_world_transfer_evidence.transfer_question: Does the local change signal identify a better or worse candidate?

domain_world_transfer_evidence.transfer_question: Does the shortest discovered procedure generalize by induction?

domain_world_transfer_evidence.transfer_question: Does a lifted coordinate explain residuals across domains without overfitting?

domain_world_transfer_evidence.falsifies_if: refined samples reject the local linear or curvature estimate

domain_world_transfer_evidence.transfer_question: Does composing paths match composing symbolic transformations?

domain_world_transfer_evidence.transfer_question: Can finite differences explain future state transitions across trajectories?

falsification_tests: swap route order and check whether the final marks differ

authored_equation_extensions.base_expression: allowed transforms preserve a reading pattern while changing coordinates

falsification_tests: apply a new transform and require the reading pattern to survive

authored_equation_extensions.base_expression: next state is better explained by a repeated residual update than by repeating the last step

falsification_tests: roll the one-step residual update across a longer held-out trace
authored_equation_extensions.why_it_goes_further: turns a local observed relation into a reusable equation grammar with explicit proof and falsification pressure

authored_equation_extensions.base_expression: piecewise moves compose into the same final state as the combined move

math_domain_curriculum.curriculum_policy: each domain is a pressure source, not an answer bank; a candidate concept must be rediscovered from observations, transferred across at least one bridge, and survive a named falsifier

domains.name: arithmetic and quantity
observation_tasks: count stable objects under permutation
compare more, fewer, same, zero, and one-more transitions
compose combine/split actions and check whether totals agree
domains.world_seed: finite objects that can appear, disappear, combine, and split

domains.name: algebra and symbolic equations
observation_tasks: solve missing values from balanced transformations
compose and invert operations
compare equivalent expressions on held-out substitutions
domains.world_seed: unknown quantities hidden behind reversible operations

domains.name: geometry and measurement
observation_tasks: measure invariant distance under translation and rotation
infer centers, axes, and boundaries from observations
compare coordinate descriptions of the same shape
domains.world_seed: points, distances, angles, boundaries, and coordinate changes

domains.name: calculus and change
observation_tasks: estimate velocity, acceleration, and curvature from finite differences
compare accumulated change against endpoint differences
separate smooth trends from boundary events
domains.world_seed: smooth and abrupt change sampled over time

domains.name: probability and uncertainty
observation_tasks: estimate frequencies from repeated samples
update beliefs after evidence
distinguish noise from deterministic residual structure
domains.world_seed: stochastic events, noisy measurements, and repeated trials

domains.name: logic and proof
observation_tasks: test whether predicates partition examples
build implication chains from observed rules
search for the smallest counterexample to a claim
domains.world_seed: statements, predicates, implications, contradictions, and counterexamples

domains.name: discrete structures and graphs
observation_tasks: discover adjacency and reachability
compare path composition and shortest routes
track state-machine transitions over repeated steps
domains.world_seed: nodes, edges, paths, neighborhoods, orderings, and finite state transitions

domains.name: symmetry and invariance
observation_tasks: apply translations, rotations, reflections, and relabelings
detect quantities unchanged by transformations
separate true invariants from coordinate artifacts
domains.world_seed: transformations that preserve or break structure

domains.name: optimization and extrema
observation_tasks: compare candidate rules by residual/error cost
search local and global optima
discover constraints and active boundaries
domains.world_seed: actions with costs, rewards, constraints, and tradeoffs

domains.name: dynamics and systems
observation_tasks: infer residual fields after a simple transition baseline
discover attractors, rotations, periodic forcing, and conservation
compose multiple laws and test held-out trajectories
domains.world_seed: interacting objects with hidden forces, phases, feedback, and conservation

domains.name: information and computation
observation_tasks: compare descriptions by compression and prediction cost
infer hidden state from partial observations
learn procedures that generalize across input sizes
domains.world_seed: messages, encodings, compression, decision trees, and algorithms

domains.name: higher-dimensional worlds
observation_tasks: invent new coordinates when residuals cannot be expressed in visible axes
project high-dimensional structure into observable views
test whether learned laws survive dimension changes
domains.world_seed: latent axes, projections, manifolds, and arbitrary-dimensional coordinates

transfer_bridges.bridge_principle: counts become variables and operations become reusable transformations
transfer_bridges.falsifier: a substitution that preserves counts breaks the symbolic equation
transfer_bridges.transfer_question: Does a counting rule still hold when the count is hidden behind an unknown?

transfer_bridges.bridge_principle: equations define coordinate constraints and geometric loci
transfer_bridges.falsifier: two algebraically equivalent forms produce different measured shapes
transfer_bridges.transfer_question: Can equivalent expressions describe the same shape under a coordinate change?

transfer_bridges.bridge_principle: local geometric differences become rates and curvature
transfer_bridges.falsifier: refined samples reject the local linear or curvature estimate
transfer_bridges.transfer_question: Does a small spatial displacement predict the observed change rate?

transfer_bridges.bridge_principle: rates compose into state transition laws
transfer_bridges.falsifier: held-out trajectories keep residual structure after the rate law is applied
transfer_bridges.transfer_question: Can finite differences explain future state transitions across trajectories?

transfer_bridges.bridge_principle: uncertainty becomes description length and inference cost
transfer_bridges.falsifier: a probabilistically better model has worse held-out description cost
transfer_bridges.transfer_question: Does the lower-uncertainty model also compress future observations better?

transfer_bridges.bridge_principle: proof obligations and counterexamples govern every promoted rule
transfer_bridges.falsifier: the claim cannot produce a setting that would break it
transfer_bridges.transfer_question: Does the rule name a domain, a falsifier, and a counterexample search?

transfer_bridges.bridge_principle: path and state composition behave like algebraic operations
transfer_bridges.falsifier: path composition is order-sensitive but the algebraic rule treats it as commutative
transfer_bridges.transfer_question: Does composing paths match composing symbolic transformations?

transfer_bridges.bridge_principle: geometric facts are promoted only if they survive allowed transformations
transfer_bridges.falsifier: the law succeeds only in the original coordinate frame
transfer_bridges.transfer_question: Does the geometric law survive rotation, reflection, translation, or relabeling?

transfer_bridges.bridge_principle: extrema are found by comparing local change directions under constraints
transfer_bridges.falsifier: following the predicted improvement direction raises error on a holdout
transfer_bridges.transfer_question: Does the local change signal identify a better or worse candidate?

transfer_bridges.bridge_principle: unexplained deterministic residuals become noise models only after structure fails
transfer_bridges.falsifier: phase, position, or hidden-state features predict the residual better than noise
transfer_bridges.transfer_question: Is the residual random after known dynamics, or still structured?

transfer_bridges.bridge_principle: short programs need proof obligations before they become laws
transfer_bridges.falsifier: a larger held-out input breaks the compressed procedure
transfer_bridges.transfer_question: Does the shortest discovered procedure generalize by induction?

transfer_bridges.bridge_principle: new latent axes are allowed only when they improve transfer or falsification
transfer_bridges.falsifier: the latent axis helps one run but fails same-structure holdouts
transfer_bridges.transfer_question: Does a lifted coordinate explain residuals across domains without overfitting?
